summary: The Rwanda Revenue Authority (RRA) operates an e-tax system that enables online filing and payment of major taxes including income tax, VAT, and customs duties. The system has achieved significant digitization for formal sector taxpayers, with electronic billing machines (EBMs) mandated for VAT-registered businesses. However, adoption among small and informal sector businesses remains limited, and digital literacy constraints affect usage rates outside major urban centers.
notes: RRA has made strong progress in digitizing tax administration, with the e-tax portal handling the majority of formal sector filings. Integration with Irembo (the national e-government platform) has improved accessibility. Mobile-based tax payment options via MTN Mobile Money and Airtel Money have expanded reach. The tax-to-GDP ratio continues to improve as digital systems broaden the tax base.
